twitter_thread: 83% of AI models are trained on datasets over 2 years old. Only 17% use human feedback.
Uncertainty sampling can reduce dataset size by 30%. Try it with active learning.
Hugging Face's Transformers simplified RLHF for 75% of our use cases.
Don't waste human feedback on 90% error correction. Use it to update model weights instead.
Online learning beats batched feedback: 25% less concept drift in our tests.
Batching human feedback can lead to 40% decrease in model accuracy.
What's your experience with human feedback in AI models? #humanaifuture #rlhf
pack.linkedin: Only 17% of AI models use human feedback effectively, while 83% rely on outdated datasets. By leveraging techniques like uncertainty sampling, which can reduce dataset size by 30%, and tools like Hugging Face's Transformers, you can simplify the RLHF process. However, beware of using human feedback solely for error correction, which can waste 90% of its potential. What lessons have you learned from implementing human feedback in your own AI projects?
tiktok_hooks: Your AI is 2 years behind if it doesn't use human feedback
Most AI models ignore 90% of human feedback
I reduced my dataset size by 30% with one simple trick
pack.reddit_headline: Is 17% the new normal for human feedback in AI?
